title: vigna unguiculata subsp. tenuis
intro: Vigna unguiculata subsp. tenuis, commonly known as the cowpea or black-eyed pea, is a sub-species of the Vigna unguiculata plant, native to Africa and widely cultivated in tropical and subtropical regions. The cowpea has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of cowpea include reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and supporting weight management, while also providing essential nutrients like protein, fiber, and vitamins. Cowpea is often prepared as a herbal tea, by infusing dried cowpea leaves in hot water, or as a powder, and is also used in traditional dishes, such as stews and soups.
